Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I should recall the standard steps in P2P Normally the process starts with creating a purchase requisition which is then approved Once approved a purchase order PO is issued to the vendor The vendor then delivers goods or services and the company receives them After that the vendor sends an invoice which needs to be matched with the PO and the goods received this is called three way matching Finally payment is released and the case is closed

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets go through each case one by one

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 looks normal Requisition created and approved on the same day then PO issued Goods received the next day invoice comes in matched payment released and closed All in order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Hmm here the PO is issued before the requisition is approved Thats a problem Normally the PO shouldnt be issued until the requisition is approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then payment is released before the goods are received and the invoice is matched Thats definitely out of order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also the invoice comes in on the same day as the goods but the payment is released immediately That skips the matching step properly

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So this case has multiple issues PO before approval payment before goods and invoice and possibly skipping or misordering the matching

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 The payment is released on Feb 8th but the invoice is received on the 9th Thats impossible because you cant release payment before receiving the invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also goods are received on the 10th which is after the payment So payment is happening before goods and invoice which is a big red flag The normal sequence is receive goods get invoice match then pay

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 The goods are received on Feb 6th but the PO is issued on the 7th Thats backwards You cant receive goods before the PO is issued The PO must be sent to the vendor before they can deliver goods

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So this case is missing the PO before goods received Thats a major anomaly

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So summarizing the anomalies Case 1002 PO issued before requisition approval payment before goods and invoice and incorrect order of activities Case 1003 Payment before invoice and goods received which is impossible Case 1004 Goods received before PO was issued

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: These are all violating the standard P2P process flow Each of these sequences could lead to issues like unauthorized purchases payment without verification or receiving goods without a prior order which are all problematic from a compliance financial and operational standpoint
